FR-001.title: Component rendering applies only in living-spec mode; feature specs render identically

FR-002.title: Unrecognized markdown falls through to the current renderer losslessly

FR-003.title: A component that throws hands its region to the existing renderer

FR-004.title: Every component preserves exact authored wording and document order

FR-005.title: Every component preserves source-line identity so comments behave unchanged

FR-006.title: Draft notice makes a draft unmistakable and preserves draft-marker detection

FR-007.title: Purpose callout renders only when a purpose section exists; never placeholder

FR-008.title: Requirement card keyed on exact heading text with no normalization
tests: webview/src/spec-viewer/markdown/livingComponents.test.ts::keys the card on the exact heading text

FR-009.title: Confidence shown only when stated; no badge on untagged requirements

FR-010.title: Inferred reads as less trustworthy without becoming a wall of warnings

FR-011.title: Per-requirement coverage shown when determinable; never rendered as zero

FR-012.title: Scenario steps separate conditions from outcomes without reorder/reword

FR-013.title: A requirement with no scenarios renders cleanly with no empty container

FR-014.title: Uncovered section opens with a count and scope statement before files

FR-015.title: Uncovered files grouped by reason for omission, not one flat list

FR-016.title: Uncovered disclosures are keyboard accessible and closed by default

FR-017.title: A spec that read everything says so plainly with no empty banner

FR-018.title: Uncovered section parsed defensively; unrecognized content falls back, no dropped lines
tests: webview/src/spec-viewer/markdown/livingComponents.test.ts::falls back to plain markdown for an unrecognized flat list

FR-019.title: Undeterminable counts omitted, never guessed, never shown as zero

FR-020.title: No document content interpolated into HTML attributes via string-built markup

FR-021.title: Accessibility-description targets use a visually-hidden class, not hidden

FR-022.title: All themes and modes supported via existing viewer tokens, incl reduced-motion

FR-023.title: Stories cover every enumerated component state

context: constraint: no document content interpolated into HTML attributes via string-built markup
constraint: preserve source-line data-line identity so line-comments keep working
constraint: fall back to current renderer, never fail the page
intent: Render a living spec's repeating structures (draft notice, purpose, requirement cards, scenarios, uncovered evidence) as components inside the existing viewer so readers can scan a draft before reading it, while ordinary markdown renders unchanged
expectations: No new reader UI or product surface — works inside the viewer we already ship
No change to how living specs are authored
Not an editing change — authored wording and document order are preserved exactly
approach: Render living-spec repeating structures as preprocessor components inside the existing webview string-render pipeline, gated to living mode by a setLivingMode flag so feature specs are byte-identical. Each component emits recognized HTML wrapped in a per-region fallback that hands a failing region back to the base renderer, preserving data-line comment identity throughout.

decisions.decision: Build components as string preprocessors in the existing render loop, not Preact for the body
decisions.why: Line-comment data-line identity, per-region fallback, and feature-spec byte parity all live in the current string loop
decisions.rejected: A Preact component tree for the living body, which would fork the comment/anchor system and the fallback contract

decisions.decision: Gate all living preprocessors on a module-level livingMode flag set from navState.livingMode
decisions.why: Feature specs must render identically to today (SC-001) and the extension already owns living-ness
decisions.rejected: Re-deriving living-ness from document content in the webview

decisions.decision: Per-component safe() fallback returns the input region unchanged on throw
decisions.why: FR-003 wants a region to fall back, not the whole document; keeps data-line stamping and drops no lines
decisions.rejected: One document-wide try/catch that would strip component treatment from everything on any single failure

decisions.decision: Requirement card wraps still-individual commentable lines, keyed on exact heading text
decisions.why: FR-008 needs the exact fold-back key and FR-005 needs per-line comment parity
decisions.rejected: Collapsing each requirement into one component-line, which loses per-line comments

decisions.decision: Gate the living preprocessors behind a module-level livingMode flag and register living-* classes in one pass-through check (line.includes("class=\"living-"))
decisions.why: Keeps feature-spec output byte-identical (SC-001) and lets all five components pass through the render loop with one recognizer edit
decisions.rejected: A per-component class list in the recognizer

decisions.decision: Requirement cards emit open/close wrapper divs and leave the heading + prose + scenarios as markdown lines the main loop renders
decisions.why: Preserves per-line .line identity so line comments survive inside a card (FR-005), instead of collapsing the block into one HTML unit
decisions.rejected: Rendering the whole card as a single pre-built HTML string

decisions.decision: Only the reason-grouped uncovered format (scope prose + bold reason bullets with nested files) is componentized; flat lists fall back to plain markdown
decisions.why: FR-015 wants grouping by reason and FR-019 forbids inventing reasons; grouping a flat list would guess. Fallback keeps every line (FR-018)
decisions.rejected: Forcing every uncovered list into a single synthetic group

decisions.decision: Lift the [inferred] tag out of prose into a confidence badge rather than leaving it inline
decisions.why: Matches the existing task [P]/[US#] marker-to-chip precedent; [inferred] is a metadata annotation, not sentence prose
decisions.rejected: Leaving [inferred] inline and duplicating it as a badge

plan.summary: Plan + research + data-model + contracts for rendering living-spec structures as gated preprocessor components in the existing webview pipeline
plan.key_finding: The body is a string-render loop, not Preact; comment identity, fallback, and feature-spec byte parity all depend on staying in that loop, so components are gated preprocessors not a component tree

tasks.summary: 32 tasks across 6 phases (Setup, Foundational gate/module/fallback, US1 draft+purpose+fallback, US2 requirement cards+scenarios, US3 uncovered evidence, Polish), waved by dependency

implement.summary: Living-spec draft notice, purpose callout, requirement cards (confidence/coverage), WHEN/THEN/AND scenarios, and uncovered-evidence disclosures now render as gated components in the viewer; feature specs unchanged, throwing components fall back per-region.
